1	"""
2	Core data models for the Noosphere knowledge system.
3	
4	Every model is a Pydantic BaseModel for serialization, validation, and
5	JSON round-tripping to disk. The fundamental unit is the Claim — an atomic
6	proposition extracted from a transcript, attributed to a speaker, and
7	positioned in embedding space.
8	"""
9	
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	from datetime import date, datetime
12	from enum import Enum
13	from typing import Optional, Any, Literal, NewType
14	from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, ConfigDict
15	import uuid
16	
17	
18	# ── Enums ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19	
20	class RelationType(str, Enum):
21	    """Semantic relationship between two claims or principles."""
22	    SUPPORTS = "supports"           # A provides evidence/reasoning for B
23	    CONTRADICTS = "contradicts"     # A is logically incompatible with B
24	    REFINES = "refines"             # A is a more precise version of B
25	    INSTANTIATES = "instantiates"   # A is a specific case of general B
26	    EXTENDS = "extends"             # A adds new scope to B
27	    ANALOGIZES = "analogizes"       # A draws structural parallel to B
28	    PRESUPPOSES = "presupposes"     # A requires B to be true
29	    QUALIFIES = "qualifies"         # A limits or conditions B
30	
31	
32	class Discipline(str, Enum):
33	    """Knowledge domains tracked by Theseus."""
34	    PHILOSOPHY = "Philosophy"
35	    PHYSICS = "Physics"
36	    AI = "AI"
37	    ENTREPRENEURSHIP = "Entrepreneurship"
38	    VC = "VC"
39	    ART = "Art & Film"
40	    LITERATURE = "Literature"
41	    MATHEMATICS = "Mathematics"
42	    ECONOMICS = "Economics"
43	    HISTORY = "History"
44	    EPISTEMOLOGY = "Epistemology"
45	    ETHICS = "Ethics"
46	    POLITICAL_PHILOSOPHY = "Political Philosophy"
47	    STRATEGY = "Strategy"
48	
49	
50	class ConvictionLevel(str, Enum):
51	    """How strongly a principle is held, inferred from discourse patterns."""
52	    AXIOM = "axiom"                 # Foundational, never questioned
53	    STRONG = "strong"               # Consistently asserted with emphasis
54	    MODERATE = "moderate"           # Asserted but open to refinement
55	    EXPLORATORY = "exploratory"     # Tentatively proposed, being tested
56	    CONTESTED = "contested"         # Actively debated among founders
57	
58	
59	# ── Round 3: Freshness & Decay (defined early for use in existing models) ────
60	
61	class Freshness(str, Enum):
62	    """Freshness status for revalidation tracking."""
63	    FRESH = "fresh"
64	    AGING = "aging"
65	    STALE = "stale"
66	    RETIRED = "retired"
67	
68	
69	# ── Pre-existing enums (needed by store / coherence) ────────────────────────
70	
71	class CoherenceVerdict(str, Enum):
72	    COHERE = "cohere"
73	    CONTRADICT = "contradict"
74	    UNRESOLVED = "unresolved"
75	
76	
77	class ConfidenceTier(str, Enum):
78	    FOUNDER = "founder"
79	    HIGH = "high"
80	    MODERATE = "moderate"
81	    LOW = "low"
82	    SPECULATIVE = "speculative"
83	
84	
85	class AdversarialChallengeStatus(str, Enum):
86	    PENDING = "pending"
87	    EVALUATED = "evaluated"
88	    ADDRESSED = "addressed"
89	    FATAL = "fatal"
90	    SURVIVED = "survived"
91	    FALLEN = "fallen"
92	
93	
94	class PredictiveClaimStatus(str, Enum):
95	    DRAFT = "draft"
96	    ACTIVE = "active"
97	    RESOLVED = "resolved"
98	    EXPIRED = "expired"
99	
100	
101	# ── Pre-existing data models (needed by store / coherence / adversarial) ────
102	
103	
104	class SixLayerScore(BaseModel):
105	    consistency: float = 0.0

111	
112	
113	class Artifact(BaseModel):
114	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))

129	
130	class Chunk(BaseModel):

141	    aggregator_verdict: CoherenceVerdict = CoherenceVerdict.UNRESOLVED
142	    prior_scores: SixLayerScore = Field(default_factory=SixLayerScore)

173	
174	
175	class AdversarialChallenge(BaseModel):
176	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
177	    conclusion_id: str = ""

182	
183	
184	class VoiceProfile(BaseModel):
185	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))

187	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)

189	
190	
191	class VoicePhaseRecord(BaseModel):
192	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))

194	
195	
196	class CitationRecord(BaseModel):
197	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
198	    firm_claim_id: str = ""
199	    voice_id: str = ""
200	
201	
202	class RelativePositionMap(BaseModel):
203	    conclusion_id: str = ""
204	
205	
206	class ReadingQueueEntry(BaseModel):
207	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
208	    status: str = "queued"
209	    rationale: str = ""
210	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
211	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
212	
213	
214	class PredictiveClaim(BaseModel):
215	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
216	    author_key: Optional[str] = ""
217	    artifact_id: Optional[str] = ""
218	    status: PredictiveClaimStatus = PredictiveClaimStatus.DRAFT
219	    source_claim_id: str = ""
220	    voice_id: str = ""
221	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
222

224	class PredictionResolution(BaseModel):

230	def voice_canonical_key(name: str) -> str:
231	    return name.lower().strip().replace(" ", "_")
232	
233	
234	# ── Core Data Models ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
235	
236	class Speaker(BaseModel):
237	    """A participant in the podcast."""
238	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
239	    name: str
240	    role: str = "founder"  # founder | guest | moderator
241	
242	
243	class TranscriptSegment(BaseModel):
244	    """A contiguous segment of speech from one speaker."""
245	    speaker: Speaker
246	    text: str
247	    start_time: Optional[float] = None   # seconds from episode start
248	    end_time: Optional[float] = None
249	    episode_id: str = ""
250	
251	
252	class Claim(BaseModel):
253	    """
254	    An atomic proposition extracted from a transcript segment.
255	
256	    This is the fundamental unit of analysis. A claim is a single
257	    assertoric sentence that can be true or false, attributed to a
258	    speaker, and positioned in embedding space.
259	    """
260	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
261	    text: str                                   # The proposition itself
262	    speaker: Speaker                            # Who said it
263	    episode_id: str                             # Which episode
264	    episode_date: date                          # When
265	    segment_context: str = ""                   # Surrounding paragraph for disambiguation
266	    disciplines: list[Discipline] = []          # Relevant domains
267	    embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None     # SBERT embedding vector
268	    confidence: float = 1.0                     # Extraction confidence (0-1)
269	    timestamp_seconds: Optional[float] = None   # Position in episode
270	    source_id: str = ""                         # Source artifact or chunk ID
271	    voice_id: str = ""                          # Voice profile ID
272	    # Round 3 additions
273	    freshness: Freshness = Freshness.FRESH
274	    last_validated_at: Optional[datetime] = None
275	
276	
277	class Principle(BaseModel):
278	    """
279	    A distilled, stable belief held by the firm.
280	
281	    Principles are derived from clusters of claims across multiple episodes.
282	    They represent the firm's enduring intellectual commitments — the things
283	    the founders believe deeply enough to act on repeatedly.
284	    """
285	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
286	    text: str                                   # Canonical statement
287	    description: str = ""                       # Elaborated explanation
288	    disciplines: list[Discipline] = []
289	    conviction: ConvictionLevel = ConvictionLevel.MODERATE
290	    conviction_score: float = 0.5               # Continuous 0-1
291	    embedding: Optional[list[float]] = None
292	
293	    # Evidence trail
294	    supporting_claims: list[str] = []           # Claim IDs
295	    first_appeared: Optional[date] = None
296	    last_reinforced: Optional[date] = None
297	    mention_count: int = 0
298	
299	    # Coherence metrics (from the 6-layer engine)
300	    coherence_score: Optional[float] = None     # Composite Coh(Γ)
301	    consistency_score: Optional[float] = None   # S₁: formal consistency
302	    argumentation_score: Optional[float] = None # S₂: grounded extension ratio
303	    probabilistic_score: Optional[float] = None # S₃: Roche's measure
304	    geometric_score: Optional[float] = None     # S₄: embedding coherence
305	    compression_score: Optional[float] = None   # S₅: information-theoretic
306	
307	    # Metadata
308	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
309	    updated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
310	    tags: list[str] = []
311	
312	
313	class Relationship(BaseModel):
314	    """A directed edge in the knowledge graph."""
315	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
316	    source_id: str                              # Principle or Claim ID
317	    target_id: str
318	    relation: RelationType

320	    evidence: str = ""                          # Why this relationship exists
321	    detected_by: str = "extraction"             # extraction | coherence | geometric | manual
322	    created_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
323	
324	
325	class Episode(BaseModel):
326	    """Metadata for a single podcast episode."""
327	    id: str = Field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
328	    number: int
329	    date: date
330	    title: str = ""

333	    speakers: list[Speaker] = []
334	    claim_count: int = 0

336	    reinforced_principles: list[str] = []       # Principle IDs mentioned again
337	
338	
339	class CoherenceReport(BaseModel):
340	    """Output of the 6-layer coherence engine on a set of principles."""
341	    principle_ids: list[str]
342	    composite_score: float                      # Coh(Γ) ∈ [0, 1]
343	    layer_scores: dict[str, float]              # S₁ through S₆
344	    contradictions_found: list[tuple[str, str, float]] = []  # (id_a, id_b, severity)
345	    weakest_links: list[str] = []               # Principle IDs with lowest support
346	    generated_at: datetime = Field(default_factory=datetime.now)
347

359	    query: InferenceQuery

362	    principles_used: list[str] = []             # Principle IDs grounding the answer
363	    confidence: float = 0.0                     # How well-grounded in principles

418	class MethodType(str, Enum):

0	"""
1	Orchestrator for the Noosphere system — The Brain of the Firm.
2	
3	This module ties all sub-modules together into a coherent pipeline.
4	The critical architectural principle: the brain stores ONLY methodological
5	knowledge (how to think). Substantive conclusions (what the founders think
6	is true) are routed to a separate Conclusions Registry for calibration.
7	
8	Pipeline:
9	  Transcript → Parse → Extract Claims → Classify (method/substance) →
10	  Route methodological claims to Ontology Graph →
11	  Route substantive claims to Conclusions Registry →
12	  Run meta-analysis on substantive discourse →
13	  Promote methodological observations to the Graph →
14	  Track temporal evolution → Run coherence checks → Persist
15	
16	All sub-module constructors and method names match their actual
17	implementations as of 2026-04-11.
18	"""
19	
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import json
23	import os
24	import uuid
25	from collections import defaultdict
26	from datetime import date, datetime
27	from pathlib import Path
28	from typing import Optional, Any
29	
30	import numpy as np
31	import structlog.contextvars
32	
33	from noosphere.models import (
34	    Claim, Discipline, Episode, InferenceQuery, InferenceResult,
35	    Principle, CoherenceReport, TemporalSnapshot, ConvictionLevel,
36	    Speaker, Relationship, RelationType, FounderProfile,
37	    InputSource, InputSourceType,
38	)
39	
40	from noosphere.config import get_settings
41	from noosphere.observability import configure_logging, get_logger
42	
43	logger = get_logger(__name__)
44	
45	
46	# ── Default Configuration ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
47	
48	DEFAULT_CONFIG = {
49	    "model_name": "",  # filled from settings when empty
50	    "model_device": "cpu",
51	    "coherence_weights": {
52	        "s1_consistency": 0.25,
53	        "s2_argumentation": 0.10,
54	        "s3_probabilistic": 0.15,
55	        "s4_geometric": 0.15,
56	        "s5_compression": 0.10,
57	        "s6_llm_judge": 0.25,
58	    },
59	    "clustering_distance_threshold": 0.3,
60	    "min_observation_confidence_for_promotion": 0.75,
61	}
62	
63	
64	class NoosphereOrchestrator:
65	    """
66	    Central orchestrator for the Noosphere knowledge system.
67	
68	    The orchestrator owns two stores:
69	      1. The Ontology Graph (methodological principles only)
70	      2. The Conclusions Registry (substantive claims for calibration)
71	
72	    The Discourse Classifier determines which store receives each claim.
73	    The Meta-Analytical Layer generates methodological observations from
74	    substantive discourse and promotes the best ones to the Graph.
75	    """
76	
77	    def __init__(self, data_dir: str | None = None):
78	        settings = get_settings()
79	        configure_logging(level=settings.log_level, json_format=True)
80	        base = Path(data_dir) if data_dir else settings.data_dir
81	        self.data_dir = base
82	        self.data_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
83	
84	        # Config
85	        self.config_path = self.data_dir / "noosphere_config.json"
86	        self.config = self._load_config()
87	        if not self.config.get("model_name"):
88	            self.config["model_name"] = settings.embedding_model_name
89	        if not self.config.get("model_device"):
90	            self.config["model_device"] = settings.embedding_device
91	
92	        # Shared SBERT model (loaded once, used everywhere)
93	        self._model = None  # lazy
94	
95	        # Sub-modules (all lazy-loaded)
96	        self._graph = None
97	        self._persistence = None
98	        self._distiller = None
99	        self._ingester = None
100	        self._classifier = None
101	        self._conclusions = None
102	        self._meta_analyzer = None
103	        self._monitor = None
104	        self._geometry = None
105	        self._founder_registry = None
106	        self._source_registry = None
107	        self._written_processor = None
108	        self._founder_analyser = None
109	        self._synthesis_engine = None
110	        self._research_advisor = None
111	
112	        # Temporal state
113	        self.snapshots: list[TemporalSnapshot] = []
114	        self.episodes: dict[str, Episode] = {}
115	        self._store = None
116	
117	        # Load existing graph if available
118	        self._load_existing_graph()
119	
120	        logger.info("noosphere_orchestrator_initialized", data_dir=str(self.data_dir))
121	
122	    # ── Config ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
123	
124	    def _load_config(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
125	        if self.config_path.exists():
126	            with open(self.config_path, "r") as f:
127	                return json.load(f)
128	        self._save_config(DEFAULT_CONFIG)
129	        return DEFAULT_CONFIG.copy()
130	
131	    def _save_config(self, config: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
132	        with open(self.config_path, "w") as f:
133	            json.dump(config, f, indent=2)
134	
135	    # ── Lazy-loaded sub-modules ──────────────────────────────────────────────
136	
137	    @property
138	    def model(self):
139	        """Shared SBERT model, loaded once."""
140	        if self._model is None:
141	            from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer
142	            name = self.config.get("model_name") or get_settings().embedding_model_name
143	            device = self.config.get("model_device", "cpu")
144	            logger.info("loading_sbert", model=name)
145	            self._model = SentenceTransformer(name, device=device)
146	        return self._model
147	
148	    @property
149	    def graph(self):
150	        """The Ontology Graph — stores methodological principles only."""
151	        if self._graph is None:
152	            from noosphere.ontology import OntologyGraph
153	            self._graph = OntologyGraph()
154	        return self._graph
155	
156	    @property
157	    def persistence(self):
158	        """Graph persistence layer."""
159	        if self._persistence is None:
160	            from noosphere.ontology import GraphPersistence
161	            self._persistence = GraphPersistence(self.graph)
162	        return self._persistence
163	
164	    @property
165	    def distiller(self):
166	        """Principle distiller — clusters claims into principles."""
167	        if self._distiller is None:
168	            from noosphere.ontology import PrincipleDistiller
169	            self._distiller = PrincipleDistiller(graph=self.graph)
170	        return self._distiller
171	
172	    @property
173	    def ingester(self):
174	        """Transcript ingester — parses and extracts claims."""
175	        if self._ingester is None:
176	            from noosphere.ingester import TranscriptIngester
177	            self._ingester = TranscriptIngester()
178	        return self._ingester
179	
180	    @property
181	    def classifier(self):
182	        """Discourse classifier — separates methodology from substance."""
183	        if self._classifier is None:
184	            from noosphere.classifier import DiscourseClassifier
185	            self._classifier = DiscourseClassifier()
186	        return self._classifier
187	
188	    @property
189	    def conclusions(self):
190	        """Conclusions registry — tracks substantive claims for calibration."""
191	        if self._conclusions is None:
192	            from noosphere.conclusions import ConclusionsRegistry
193	            path = str(self.data_dir / "conclusions_registry.json")
194	            self._conclusions = ConclusionsRegistry(data_path=path)
195	        return self._conclusions
196	
197	    @property
198	    def store(self):
199	        """SQLite store (claims, conclusions, drift, coherence cache)."""
200	        if self._store is None:
201	            from noosphere.store import Store
202	
203	            self._store = Store.from_database_url(get_settings().database_url)
204	        return self._store
205	
206	    @property
207	    def meta_analyzer(self):
208	        """Meta-analytical layer — extracts methodology from substance."""
209	        if self._meta_analyzer is None:
210	            from noosphere.meta_analysis import MetaAnalyzer
211	            self._meta_analyzer = MetaAnalyzer(
212	                conclusions_registry=self.conclusions
213	            )
214	        return self._meta_analyzer
215	
216	    @property
217	    def geometry(self):
218	        """Embedding geometry analyzer."""
219	        if self._geometry is None:
220	            from noosphere.geometry import EmbeddingAnalyzer
221	            self._geometry = EmbeddingAnalyzer()
222	        return self._geometry
223	
224	    @property
225	    def founder_registry(self):
226	        """Founder registry — maps speakers to stable profiles."""
227	        if self._founder_registry is None:
228	            from noosphere.founders import FounderRegistry
229	            path = str(self.data_dir / "founders_registry.json")
230	            self._founder_registry = FounderRegistry(data_path=path)
231	        return self._founder_registry
232	
233	    @property
234	    def source_registry(self):
235	        """Input source registry — provenance for all ingested material."""
236	        if self._source_registry is None:
237	            from noosphere.founders import InputSourceRegistry
238	            path = str(self.data_dir / "input_sources.json")
239	            self._source_registry = InputSourceRegistry(data_path=path)
240	        return self._source_registry
241	
242	    @property
243	    def written_processor(self):
244	        """Processor for written inputs (essays, memos, notes)."""
245	        if self._written_processor is None:
246	            from noosphere.founders import WrittenInputProcessor
247	            self._written_processor = WrittenInputProcessor(
248	                founder_registry=self.founder_registry,
249	                source_registry=self.source_registry,
250	            )
251	        return self._written_processor
252	
253	    @property
254	    def founder_analyser(self):
255	        """Analytical engine for inter-founder dynamics."""
256	        if self._founder_analyser is None:
257	            from noosphere.founders import FounderAnalyser
258	            self._founder_analyser = FounderAnalyser(
259	                registry=self.founder_registry,
260	            )
261	        return self._founder_analyser
262	
263	    @property
264	    def synthesis(self):
265	        """Post-discussion synthesis engine."""
266	        if self._synthesis_engine is None:
267	            from noosphere.synthesis import SynthesisEngine
268	            self._synthesis_engine = SynthesisEngine(
269	                data_dir=self.data_dir,
270	                graph=self.graph,
271	                geometry=self.geometry,
272	                model=self.model,
273	                founder_registry=self.founder_registry,
274	                conclusions_registry=self.conclusions,
275	            )
276	        return self._synthesis_engine
277	
278	    @property
279	    def research_advisor(self):
280	        """Post-discussion research advisor — topic and reading suggestions."""
281	        if self._research_advisor is None:
282	            from noosphere.research_advisor import ResearchAdvisor
283	            self._research_advisor = ResearchAdvisor(
284	                data_dir=self.data_dir,
285	                graph=self.graph,
286	                model=self.model,
287	                conclusions_registry=self.conclusions,
288	            )
289	        return self._research_advisor
290	
291	    # ── Persistence ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
292	
293	    def _load_existing_graph(self) -> None:
294	        graph_path = self.data_dir / "graph.json"
295	        if graph_path.exists():
296	            try:
297	                from noosphere.ontology import GraphPersistence
298	                # Must init graph first, then persistence loads into it
299	                _ = self.graph

467	def _sqlite_migrate_literature_columns(engine: Engine) -> None:
468	    if not str(engine.url).startswith("sqlite"):
469	        return
470	    insp = inspect(engine)
471	    tables = insp.get_table_names()
472	    if "artifact" not in tables:
473	        return
474	    acols = {c["name"] for c in insp.get_columns("artifact")}
475	    with engine.begin() as conn:
476	        if "license_status" not in acols:
477	            conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE artifact ADD COLUMN license_status VARCHAR DEFAULT 'unknown'"))
478	        if "literature_connector" not in acols:
479	            conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE artifact ADD COLUMN literature_connector VARCHAR DEFAULT ''"))
480	
481	
482	def _sqlite_migrate_temporal_columns(engine: Engine) -> None:
483	    if not str(engine.url).startswith("sqlite"):
484	        return
485	    insp = inspect(engine)
486	    tables = insp.get_table_names()
487	    if "artifact" not in tables:
488	        return
489	    acols = {c["name"] for c in insp.get_columns("artifact")}
490	    with engine.begin() as conn:
491	        if "effective_at" not in acols:
492	            conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE artifact ADD COLUMN effective_at DATETIME"))
493	        if "superseded_at" not in acols:
494	            conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE artifact ADD COLUMN superseded_at DATETIME"))
495	        if "effective_at_inferred" not in acols:
496	            conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE artifact ADD COLUMN effective_at_inferred BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1"))

499	def _artifact_row_default_effective(row: StoredArtifact) -> tuple[datetime, bool]:
500	    """Return (effective_at, inferred) for a DB row missing effective_at."""
501	    if row.source_date_iso:
502	        try:
503	            d = date.fromisoformat(row.source_date_iso[:10])
504	            return (
505	                datetime(d.year, d.month, d.day, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
506	                False,
507	            )
508	        except ValueError:
509	            pass
510	    return _dt(row.created_at), True

513	def _backfill_artifact_effective_times(engine: Engine) -> None:
514	    with Session(engine) as s:
515	        rows = s.exec(select(StoredArtifact)).all()
516	        changed = False
517	        for r in rows:
518	            if getattr(r, "effective_at", None) is not None:
519	                continue
520	            eff, inf = _artifact_row_default_effective(r)
521	            r.effective_at = eff
522	            r.effective_at_inferred = inf
523	            s.add(r)
524	            changed = True
525	        if changed:
526	            s.commit()

529	def _seed_embedding_model_versions(engine: Engine) -> None:
530	    from noosphere.config import get_settings
531	
532	    with Session(engine) as s:
533	        n = len(s.exec(select(StoredEmbeddingModelVersion)).all())
534	        if n > 0:
535	            return
536	        sid = "seed-embedding-default"
537	        s.add(
538	            StoredEmbeddingModelVersion(
539	                id=sid,
540	                effective_from=datetime(2020, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
541	                model_name=get_settings().embedding_model_name,
542	                notes="Auto-seed: replace with dated rows when upgrading encoders.",
543	            )
544	        )
545	        s.commit()

554	    @classmethod
555	    def from_database_url(cls, url: str) -> Store:
556	        connect_args: dict[str, Any] = {}
557	        if url.startswith("sqlite"):
558	            connect_args["check_same_thread"] = False
559	        eng = create_engine(url, connect_args=connect_args)
560	        SQLModel.metadata.create_all(eng)

562	        _sqlite_migrate_literature_columns(eng)
563	        _backfill_artifact_effective_times(eng)
564	        _seed_embedding_model_versions(eng)
565	        return cls(eng)

3	from typing import Any, Callable

7	PreHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any], None]
8	PostHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, Any], None]
9	FailureHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, BaseException], None]
10	
11	_PRE_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, PreHook]] = []
12	_POST_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, PostHook]] = []
13	_FAILURE_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, FailureHook]] = []
14

16	def _replace_or_append(
17	    lst: list[tuple[str, Callable]], name: str, hook: Callable
18	) -> None:
19	    for i, (n, _) in enumerate(lst):
20	        if n == name:
21	            lst[i] = (name, hook)
22	            return
23	    lst.append((name, hook))
24	
25	
26	def register_pre_hook(name: str, hook: PreHook) -> None:
27	    _replace_or_append(_PRE_HOOKS, name, hook)
28	
29	
30	def register_post_hook(name: str, hook: PostHook) -> None:
31	    _replace_or_append(_POST_HOOKS, name, hook)
32	
33	
34	def register_failure_hook(name: str, hook: FailureHook) -> None:
35	    _replace_or_append(_FAILURE_HOOKS, name, hook)
36	
37	
38	def unregister_hook(name: str) -> None:
39	    for lst in (_PRE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _FAILURE_HOOKS):
40	        lst[:] = [(n, h) for n, h in lst if n != name]

1	"""Cascade post-hook: automatically emits cascade edges from method outputs.
2	
3	Convention for edge emission from method outputs:
4	
5	1. Standard methods (single source node):
6	   The method input is expected to have a `primary_src` or `node_id` field
7	   identifying the source node. The output must have `<relation>_targets`
8	   fields (e.g. `supports_targets: list[str]`) for each relation declared
9	   in the method's `emits_edges` spec.
10	
11	2. Aggregator methods (multiple sources):
12	   When the input has no single source node, the output must provide
13	   `emitted_edges: list[dict]` where each dict has keys:
14	   {src, dst, relation, confidence}.
15	"""
16	from __future__ import annotations
17	
18	import logging
19	from typing import Any
20	
21	from noosphere.models import CascadeEdgeRelation, Method, MethodInvocation
22	
23	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
24	
25	
26	class CascadeEdgeDeclarationError(Exception):
27	    """Method output schema lacks a declared <relation>_targets field."""
28	
29	
30	def _resolve_source(validated_input: Any) -> str | None:
31	    """Resolve the primary source node from a method's validated input."""
32	    if hasattr(validated_input, "primary_src"):
33	        return validated_input.primary_src
34	    if hasattr(validated_input, "node_id"):
35	        return validated_input.node_id
36	    if isinstance(validated_input, dict):
37	        return validated_input.get("primary_src") or validated_input.get("node_id")
38	    return None
39	
40	
41	def _emit_edges(
42	    spec: Method,
43	    inv: MethodInvocation,
44	    validated_input: Any,
45	    result: Any,
46	) -> None:
47	    from noosphere.cascade.graph import CascadeGraph
48	    from noosphere.methods._decorator import _get_store
49	    from noosphere.methods._registry import REGISTRY
50	
51	    declared = REGISTRY.get_emits_edges(spec.method_id)
52	    if not declared:
53	        return
54	
55	    store = _get_store()
56	    if store is None:
57	        logger.warning("No store available for cascade edge emission")
58	        return
59	
60	    # Ensure the invocation exists in the store before inserting edges,
61	    # since post-hooks fire before the decorator persists the invocation.
62	    try:
63	        store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
64	    except Exception:
65	        pass  # already persisted or duplicate — fine
66	
67	    if hasattr(result, "emitted_edges") and result.emitted_edges:
68	        graph = CascadeGraph(store)
69	        for entry in result.emitted_edges:
70	            if isinstance(entry, dict):
71	                rel = entry.get("relation", "")
72	                if isinstance(rel, str):
73	                    rel = CascadeEdgeRelation(rel)
74	                graph.add_edge(
75	                    src=entry["src"],
76	                    dst=entry["dst"],
77	                    relation=rel,
78	                    method_invocation_id=inv.id,
79	                    confidence=entry.get("confidence", 1.0),
80	                )
81	            else:
82	                graph.add_edge(
83	                    src=entry.src,
84	                    dst=entry.dst,
85	                    relation=entry.relation if isinstance(entry.relation, CascadeEdgeRelation) else CascadeEdgeRelation(entry.relation),
86	                    method_invocation_id=inv.id,
87	                    confidence=getattr(entry, "confidence", 1.0),
88	                )
89	        return
90	
91	    src = _resolve_source(validated_input)
92	    if src is None:
93	        logger.warning(
94	            "Cannot resolve source node for method %s; skipping edge emission",
95	            spec.name,
96	        )
97	        return
98	
99	    graph = CascadeGraph(store)
100	    confidence = getattr(result, "confidence", 1.0)
101	    if isinstance(confidence, property) or not isinstance(confidence, (int, float)):
102	        confidence = 1.0
103	
104	    for relation in declared:
105	        field_name = f"{relation.value}_targets"
106	        if not hasattr(result, field_name):
107	            continue
108	        targets = getattr(result, field_name)
109	        if targets is None:
110	            continue
111	        for dst in targets:
112	            graph.add_edge(
113	                src=src,
114	                dst=dst,
115	                relation=relation,
116	                method_invocation_id=inv.id,
117	                confidence=confidence,
118	            )
119	
120	
121	def check_declaration_parity(output_schema: Any, declared_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation]) -> None:
122	    """Verify that the output schema has fields matching declared edge relations.
123	
124	    Raises CascadeEdgeDeclarationError if a declared relation has no
125	    corresponding <relation>_targets field in the output schema.
126	    """
127	    if not declared_edges:
128	        return
129	
130	    if hasattr(output_schema, "model_fields"):
131	        fields = set(output_schema.model_fields.keys())
132	    elif hasattr(output_schema, "__fields__"):
133	        fields = set(output_schema.__fields__.keys())
134	    elif isinstance(output_schema, dict):
135	        props = output_schema.get("properties", {})
136	        fields = set(props.keys())
137	    else:
138	        return
139	
140	    if "emitted_edges" in fields:
141	        return
142	
143	    for relation in declared_edges:
144	        expected_field = f"{relation.value}_targets"
145	        if expected_field not in fields:
146	            raise CascadeEdgeDeclarationError(
147	                f"Output schema {output_schema!r} missing field '{expected_field}' "
148	                f"for declared edge relation '{relation.value}'"
149	            )

9	
10	from noosphere.models import Actor, ContextMeta, Method, MethodInvocation
11	from noosphere.storage_client import storage_client_from_env
12	from noosphere.ledger.keys import KeyRing
13	from noosphere.methods._decorator import _get_store
14	
15	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
16	
17	_seen_invocations: set[str] = set()
18	
19	_invocation_refs: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {}
20	
21	
22	def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:
23	    if isinstance(obj, BaseModel):
24	        obj = obj.model_dump(mode="json")
25	    return json.dumps(obj, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)
26	
27	
28	def _pre_capture_inputs(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any) -> None:
29	    if inv.id in _seen_invocations:
30	        return
31	    storage = storage_client_from_env()
32	    serialized = _canonical_json(input_data).encode()
33	    key = f"ledger/inputs/{hashlib.sha256(serialized).hexdigest()}"
34	    blob_ref = storage.put_bytes(key=key, data=serialized, content_type="application/json")
35	    _invocation_refs[inv.id] = {"inputs_ref": blob_ref}
36	
37	
38	def _post_append(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any, result: Any) -> None:
39	    if inv.id in _seen_invocations:
40	        return
41	    _seen_invocations.add(inv.id)
42	
43	    store = _get_store()
44	    if store is None:
45	        logger.warning("No store available for ledger append")
46	        return
47	
48	    storage = storage_client_from_env()
49	    serialized = _canonical_json(result).encode()
50	    key = f"ledger/outputs/{hashlib.sha256(serialized).hexdigest()}"
51	    outputs_blob_ref = storage.put_bytes(key=key, data=serialized, content_type="application/json")
52	
53	    refs = _invocation_refs.pop(inv.id, {})
54	    inputs_ref = refs.get("inputs_ref", "")
55	
56	    try:
57	        keyring = KeyRing()
58	    except Exception:
59	        logger.warning("Ledger keyring not configured, skipping append")
60	        return
61	
62	    from noosphere.ledger.ledger import Ledger
63	
64	    ledger = Ledger(store, keyring)
65	    ledger.append(
66	        actor=Actor(kind="method", id=spec.method_id, display_name=spec.name),
67	        method_id=spec.method_id,
68	        inputs_hash=inv.input_hash,
69	        outputs_hash=inv.output_hash,
70	        inputs_ref=inputs_ref,
71	        outputs_ref=outputs_blob_ref,
72	        context=ContextMeta(
73	            tenant_id=inv.tenant_id,
74	            correlation_id=inv.correlation_id,
75	        ),
76	    )
77	
78	
79	def _on_failure_append(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any, exc: BaseException) -> None:
80	    if inv.id in _seen_invocations:
81	        return
82	    _seen_invocations.add(inv.id)
83	
84	    store = _get_store()
85	    if store is None:
86	        logger.warning("No store available for ledger failure append")
87	        return
88	
89	    refs = _invocation_refs.pop(inv.id, {})
90	    inputs_ref = refs.get("inputs_ref", "")
91	
92	    try:
93	        keyring = KeyRing()
94	    except Exception:
95	        logger.warning("Ledger keyring not configured, skipping failure append")
96	        return
97	
98	    from noosphere.ledger.ledger import Ledger
99	
100	    ledger = Ledger(store, keyring)
101	    ledger.append(
102	        actor=Actor(kind="method", id=spec.method_id, display_name=spec.name),
103	        method_id=spec.method_id,
104	        inputs_hash=inv.input_hash,
105	        outputs_hash="",
106	        inputs_ref=inputs_ref,
107	        outputs_ref="",
108	        context=ContextMeta(
109	            tenant_id=inv.tenant_id,
110	            correlation_id=inv.correlation_id,
111	        ),
112	    )
113	
114	
115	def register_ledger_hooks() -> None:
116	    from noosphere.methods import register_pre_hook, register_post_hook, register_failure_hook
117	
118	    register_pre_hook("ledger.capture_inputs", _pre_capture_inputs)
119	    register_post_hook("ledger.append", _post_append)
120	    register_failure_hook("ledger.append_on_failure", _on_failure_append)

26	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

46	            return _store_factory()

48	            return None

52	def _canonical_json(obj: Any) -> str:

58	def _current_git_sha() -> str:
59	    sha = os.environ.get("THESEUS_GIT_SHA")
60	    if sha:
61	        return sha
62	    try:
63	        result = subprocess.run(
64	            ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
65	            capture_output=True,
66	            text=True,
67	            timeout=5,
68	        )
69	        if result.returncode == 0:
70	            return result.stdout.strip()
71	    except Exception:
72	        pass
73	    return "unknown"

80	def _current_tenant_id() -> str:
81	    return TENANT_ID.get()

84	def _validate_against_schema(schema: Any, data: Any) -> Any:
85	    if isinstance(schema, type) and issubclass(schema, BaseModel):
86	        if isinstance(data, schema):
87	            return data
88	        return schema.model_validate(data)
89	    if isinstance(schema, dict) and schema:
90	        try:
91	            import jsonschema as _js
92	
93	            _js.validate(data, schema)
94	        except ImportError:
95	            pass
96	    return data
97	
98	
99	def _utcnow() -> datetime:
100	    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
101	
102	
103	def _run_failure_path(
104	    spec: Method,
105	    inv: MethodInvocation,
106	    validated_input: Any,
107	    exc: BaseException,
108	) -> None:
109	    inv = inv.model_copy(
110	        update={
111	            "ended_at": _utcnow(),
112	            "succeeded": False,
113	            "error_kind": type(exc).__name__,
114	        }
115	    )
116	    for hook_name, hook in _FAILURE_HOOKS:
117	        try:
118	            hook(spec, inv, validated_input, exc)
119	        except Exception:
120	            logger.exception("Failure hook %s raised", hook_name)
121	    store = _get_store()
122	    if store is not None:
123	        try:
124	            store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
125	        except Exception:
126	            logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")

129	def register_method(
130	    *,
131	    name: str,
132	    version: str,
133	    method_type: MethodType,
134	    input_schema: Any,
135	    output_schema: Any,
136	    description: str,
137	    rationale: str,
138	    preconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
139	    postconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
140	    dependencies: list[tuple[str, str]] | tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (),
141	    owner: str,
142	    status: str = "active",
143	    nondeterministic: bool = False,
144	    emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation] | tuple[CascadeEdgeRelation, ...] = (),
145	) -> Callable:
146	    def decorator(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
147	        if isinstance(input_schema, type) and issubclass(input_schema, BaseModel):
148	            in_schema_dict = input_schema.model_json_schema()
149	        elif isinstance(input_schema, dict):
150	            in_schema_dict = input_schema
151	        else:
152	            in_schema_dict = {}
153	
154	        if isinstance(output_schema, type) and issubclass(output_schema, BaseModel):
155	            out_schema_dict = output_schema.model_json_schema()
156	        elif isinstance(output_schema, dict):
157	            out_schema_dict = output_schema
158	        else:
159	            out_schema_dict = {}
160	
161	        impl = MethodImplRef(
162	            module=fn.__module__,
163	            fn_name=fn.__qualname__,
164	            git_sha=_current_git_sha(),
165	            image_digest=None,
166	        )
167	
168	        spec_for_hash = {
169	            "name": name,
170	            "version": version,
171	            "method_type": method_type.value,
172	            "input_schema": in_schema_dict,
173	            "output_schema": out_schema_dict,
174	            "description": description,
175	            "rationale": rationale,
176	            "preconditions": list(preconditions),
177	            "postconditions": list(postconditions),
178	            "dependencies": [list(d) for d in dependencies],
179	            "implementation": impl.model_dump(mode="json"),
180	            "owner": owner,
181	            "status": status,
182	            "nondeterministic": nondeterministic,
183	        }
184	
185	        method_id = hashlib.sha256(
186	            _canonical_json(spec_for_hash).encode()
187	        ).hexdigest()[:32]
188	
189	        spec = Method(
190	            method_id=method_id,
191	            name=name,
192	            version=version,
193	            method_type=method_type,
194	            input_schema=in_schema_dict,
195	            output_schema=out_schema_dict,
196	            description=description,
197	            rationale=rationale,
198	            preconditions=list(preconditions),
199	            postconditions=list(postconditions),
200	            dependencies=list(dependencies),
201	            implementation=impl,
202	            owner=owner,
203	            status=status,
204	            nondeterministic=nondeterministic,
205	            created_at=_utcnow(),
206	        )
207	
208	        @wraps(fn)
209	        def wrapped(input_data: Any) -> Any:
210	            validated_input = _validate_against_schema(input_schema, input_data)
211	            input_hash = hashlib.sha256(
212	                _canonical_json(validated_input).encode()
213	            ).hexdigest()
214	
215	            inv = MethodInvocation(
216	                id=str(uuid4()),
217	                method_id=spec.method_id,
218	                input_hash=input_hash,
219	                output_hash="",
220	                started_at=_utcnow(),
221	                ended_at=None,
222	                succeeded=False,
223	                error_kind=None,
224	                correlation_id=_current_correlation_id(),
225	                tenant_id=_current_tenant_id(),
226	            )
227	
228	            try:
229	                for _hook_name, hook in _PRE_HOOKS:
230	                    hook(spec, inv, validated_input)
231	            except Exception as e:
232	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
233	                raise
234	
235	            try:
236	                result = fn(validated_input)
237	                _validate_against_schema(output_schema, result)
238	                output_hash = hashlib.sha256(
239	                    _canonical_json(result).encode()
240	                ).hexdigest()
241	                inv = inv.model_copy(
242	                    update={
243	                        "ended_at": _utcnow(),
244	                        "succeeded": True,
245	                        "output_hash": output_hash,
246	                    }
247	                )
248	
249	                for hook_name, hook in _POST_HOOKS:
250	                    try:
251	                        hook(spec, inv, validated_input, result)
252	                    except Exception:
253	                        logger.exception("Post-hook %s raised", hook_name)
254	
255	                store = _get_store()
256	                if store is not None:
257	                    try:
258	                        store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
259	                    except Exception:
260	                        logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
261	
262	                return result
263	            except Exception as e:
264	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
265	                raise
266	
267	        wrapped.__method_spec__ = spec  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
268	
269	        REGISTRY.register(spec, wrapped)
270	
271	        if emits_edges:
272	            REGISTRY.set_emits_edges(method_id, list(emits_edges))
273	
274	        store = _get_store()
275	        if store is not None:
276	            try:
277	                store.insert_method(spec)
278	            except Exception:
279	                logger.exception("Failed to persist method spec")
280	
281	        return wrapped
282	
283	    return decorator

880	    def get_state_as_of(self, as_of: date) -> dict[str, Any]:
881	        """
882	        Snapshot of claim / artifact ids whose ``effective_at`` is on or before ``as_of``
883	        and that are not superseded on that cutoff. Downstream replay should filter reads
884	        through this set (or ``temporal_replay.filter_claims_as_of`` on a claim dict).
885	        """
886	        from noosphere.temporal_replay import filter_claims_as_of
887	
888	        claims: dict[str, Claim] = {}
889	        for cid in self.list_claim_ids():
890	            c = self.get_claim(cid)
891	            if c is not None:
892	                claims[cid] = c
893	        filtered = filter_claims_as_of(self, claims, as_of)
894	        artifact_ids = sorted({c.source_id for c in filtered.values() if c.source_id})
895	        return {
896	            "as_of": as_of.isoformat(),
897	            "claim_count": len(filtered),
898	            "claim_ids": sorted(filtered.keys()),
899	            "artifact_ids": artifact_ids,

1710	    def insert_temporal_cut(self, cut: TemporalCut) -> None:
1711	        row = StoredTemporalCut(
1712	            cut_id=cut.cut_id, payload_json=cut.model_dump_json()
1713	        )
1714	        with self.session() as s:
1715	            s.add(row)
1716	            for outcome in cut.outcomes:
1717	                if not s.get(StoredOutcome, outcome.outcome_id):
1718	                    s.add(StoredOutcome(
1719	                        outcome_id=outcome.outcome_id,
1720	                        payload_json=outcome.model_dump_json(),
1721	                    ))
1722	                assoc_id = f"{cut.cut_id}:{outcome.outcome_id}"
1723	                if not s.get(StoredCutOutcome, assoc_id):
1724	                    s.add(StoredCutOutcome(
1725	                        id=assoc_id,
1726	                        cut_id=cut.cut_id,
1727	                        outcome_id=outcome.outcome_id,
1728	                    ))
1729	            s.commit()

## Noosphere Codebase Reference Report

### 1. Core Models (noosphere/models.py)

**Not found in models.py.** The codebase does not define an explicit `EmbargoViolation` type. Embargo logic is handled through `CorpusSelector` objects with an `embargoed` field in `TemporalCut`.

**Note:** `orchestrator_run_id` is optional and found in `ContextMeta.orchestrator_run_id`, not directly in method invocations. It's used by the ledger system to track related operations across an orchestrator run.

class Store:
    """Typed CRUD facade over SQLite."""

    def __init__(self, engine: Engine) -> None:
        self.engine = engine

    @classmethod
    def from_database_url(cls, url: str) -> Store:
        connect_args: dict[str, Any] = {}
        if url.startswith("sqlite"):
            connect_args["check_same_thread"] = False
        eng = create_engine(url, connect_args=connect_args)
        SQLModel.metadata.create_all(eng)
        # ... migrations ...
        return cls(eng)

    @contextmanager
    def session(self) -> Generator[Session, None, None]:
        with Session(self.engine) as s:
            yield s

---

### 3. Hook Registration Mechanism (noosphere/methods/_hooks.py)

#### Hook Types
```python
PreHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any], None]
PostHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, Any], None]
FailureHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, BaseException], None]
```

#### Hook Registration Functions
```python
def register_pre_hook(name: str, hook: PreHook) -> None:
    """Register a pre-hook by name (replaces if exists)."""
    
def register_post_hook(name: str, hook: PostHook) -> None:
    """Register a post-hook by name (replaces if exists)."""
    
def register_failure_hook(name: str, hook: FailureHook) -> None:
    """Register a failure hook by name (replaces if exists)."""
    
def unregister_hook(name: str) -> None:
    """Remove a hook by name (removes from all three lists)."""
```

#### Hook Invocation Flow (in _decorator.py)
1. **Pre-hooks** fire BEFORE method execution
2. **Post-hooks** fire AFTER successful execution
3. **Failure-hooks** fire on exception

Hooks have access to:
- `spec: Method` - the method specification
- `inv: MethodInvocation` - the invocation record
- `validated_input: Any` - the validated input
- `result: Any` (post-hooks only) - the method result
- `exc: BaseException` (failure-hooks only) - the exception

---

### 4. Ledger Hook System (noosphere/ledger/hooks.py)

The ledger hooks demonstrate the pattern:
```python
def _pre_capture_inputs(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any) -> None:
    """Capture and store inputs in external blob storage."""
    
def _post_append(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any, result: Any) -> None:
    """After success, append to immutable ledger and persist outputs."""
    
def _on_failure_append(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any, exc: BaseException) -> None:
    """On failure, record exception details to ledger."""

def register_ledger_hooks() -> None:
    register_pre_hook("ledger.capture_inputs", _pre_capture_inputs)
    register_post_hook("ledger.append", _post_append)
    register_failure_hook("ledger.append_on_failure", _on_failure_append)
```

---

### 5. Cascade Edge Hook System (noosphere/cascade/hooks.py)

Post-hooks automatically emit cascade edges from method outputs:
- Methods declare `emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation]`
- Output must have `<relation>_targets: list[str]` fields OR `emitted_edges: list[dict]`
- Hook automatically inserts edges into `CascadeGraph`

```python
def _emit_edges(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, validated_input: Any, result: Any) -> None:
    """Post-hook that emits cascade edges from method output."""
```

---

### 6. Methods Directory Structure (noosphere/methods/)

**Core Files:**
- `__init__.py` - Public API exports
- `_decorator.py` - `@register_method` decorator and invocation wrapper
- `_hooks.py` - Hook registration system
- `_registry.py` - Global method registry

**Implemented Methods:**
- `classify_claim_type.py` - Classification method
- `contradiction_geometry.py` - Geometric contradiction analysis
- `decompose_voice.py` - Voice/speaker decomposition
- `external_claim_match.py` - Match claims to external sources
- `extract_claims.py` - Extract claims from text
- `extract_from_corpus.py` - Extract from document corpora
- `extract_prediction.py` - Extract predictive claims
- `nli_scorer.py` - Natural language inference scoring
- `six_layer_coherence.py` - 6-layer coherence engine
- `suggest_research.py` - Research suggestion generation
- `synthesize_conclusion.py` - Synthesize conclusions

---

### 7. EmbeddingVersionPin

**Not a standalone type.** Instead, it's:
- A string field on `TemporalCut.embedding_version_pin: str`
- Used to pin the exact embedding model version for reproducible evaluation
- Maps to `StoredEmbeddingModelVersion` table for version tracking

**Embedding Model Versioning:**
```python
class StoredEmbeddingModelVersion(SQLModel, table=True):
    """Pinned embedding encoder versions for honest replay disclaimers."""
    id: str = Field(primary_key=True)
    effective_from: datetime = Field(index=True)
    model_name: str = ""
    notes: str = ""
```

The store provides `_seed_embedding_model_versions()` to initialize default versions at database creation.

---

### 8. Method Decorator Usage Pattern

```python
from noosphere.methods import register_method
from noosphere.models import MethodType

@register_method(
    name="my_method",
    version="1.0",
    method_type=MethodType.JUDGMENT,
    input_schema=MyInputModel,
    output_schema=MyOutputModel,
    description="What it does",
    rationale="Why it's designed this way",
    preconditions=["precondition1"],
    postconditions=["postcondition1"],
    dependencies=[("other_method", "1.0")],
    owner="team_name",
    status="active",
    nondeterministic=False,
    emits_edges=[CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS, CascadeEdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS],
)
def my_method(input_data: MyInputModel) -> MyOutputModel:
    # Method body
    result = ...
    result.supports_targets = [...]  # Populate for each emits_edges relation
    result.confidence = 0.9
    return result
```

The `@register_method` decorator automatically:
1. Creates a `Method` spec with hashed `method_id`
2. Wraps execution with pre/post/failure hooks
3. Validates input and output against schemas
4. Computes hashes for inputs/outputs
5. Persists `MethodInvocation` records
6. Stores method spec in the database

---

### Summary for Building Evaluation Package

**Key interfaces to use:**
1. `Store.insert_temporal_cut(cut: TemporalCut)` - Create temporal evaluation slices
2. `Store.insert_outcome(outcome: Outcome, cut_id: str)` - Associate outcomes with cuts
3. `Store.insert_counterfactual_run(run: CounterfactualEvalRun)` - Persist eval run results
4. `@register_method` decorator - Define evaluation methods with hook support
5. `register_post_hook()` - Custom post-processing for evaluation results
6. `Store.iter_temporal_cuts()` / `Store.list_outcomes_for_cut()` - Read evaluation data

**Context propagation:**
- Use `ContextMeta.orchestrator_run_id` to track related operations
- Set via `CORRELATION_ID` and `TENANT_ID` contextvars in the decorator
- Access in hooks via `inv.correlation_id` and `inv.tenant_id`

### 2. Store Class (noosphere/store.py)

#### Store Constructor and Factory
```python
class Store:
    """Typed CRUD facade over SQLite."""

    def __init__(self, engine: Engine) -> None:
        self.engine = engine

    @classmethod
    def from_database_url(cls, url: str) -> Store:
        connect_args: dict[str, Any] = {}
        if url.startswith("sqlite"):
            connect_args["check_same_thread"] = False
        eng = create_engine(url, connect_args=connect_args)
        SQLModel.metadata.create_all(eng)
        # ... migrations ...
        return cls(eng)

    @contextmanager
    def session(self) -> Generator[Session, None, None]:
        with Session(self.engine) as s:
            yield s
```

### 3. Hook Registration Mechanism (noosphere/methods/_hooks.py)

#### Hook Types
```python
PreHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any], None]
PostHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, Any], None]
FailureHook = Callable[[Method, MethodInvocation, Any, BaseException], None]
```

#### Hook Registration Functions
```python
def register_pre_hook(name: str, hook: PreHook) -> None:
    """Register a pre-hook by name (replaces if exists)."""
    
def register_post_hook(name: str, hook: PostHook) -> None:
    """Register a post-hook by name (replaces if exists)."""
    
def register_failure_hook(name: str, hook: FailureHook) -> None:
    """Register a failure hook by name (replaces if exists)."""
    
def unregister_hook(name: str) -> None:
    """Remove a hook by name (removes from all three lists)."""
```

#### Hook Invocation Flow (in _decorator.py)
1. **Pre-hooks** fire BEFORE method execution
2. **Post-hooks** fire AFTER successful execution
3. **Failure-hooks** fire on exception

Hooks have access to:
- `spec: Method` - the method specification
- `inv: MethodInvocation` - the invocation record
- `validated_input: Any` - the validated input
- `result: Any` (post-hooks only) - the method result
- `exc: BaseException` (failure-hooks only) - the exception

---

### 4. Ledger Hook System (noosphere/ledger/hooks.py)

The ledger hooks demonstrate the pattern:
```python
def _pre_capture_inputs(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any) -> None:
    """Capture and store inputs in external blob storage."""
    
def _post_append(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any, result: Any) -> None:
    """After success, append to immutable ledger and persist outputs."""
    
def _on_failure_append(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, input_data: Any, exc: BaseException) -> None:
    """On failure, record exception details to ledger."""

def register_ledger_hooks() -> None:
    register_pre_hook("ledger.capture_inputs", _pre_capture_inputs)
    register_post_hook("ledger.append", _post_append)
    register_failure_hook("ledger.append_on_failure", _on_failure_append)
```

---

### 5. Cascade Edge Hook System (noosphere/cascade/hooks.py)

Post-hooks automatically emit cascade edges from method outputs:
- Methods declare `emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation]`
- Output must have `<relation>_targets: list[str]` fields OR `emitted_edges: list[dict]`
- Hook automatically inserts edges into `CascadeGraph`

```python
def _emit_edges(spec: Method, inv: MethodInvocation, validated_input: Any, result: Any) -> None:
    """Post-hook that emits cascade edges from method output."""
```

---

### 6. Methods Directory Structure (noosphere/methods/)

**Core Files:**
- `__init__.py` - Public API exports
- `_decorator.py` - `@register_method` decorator and invocation wrapper
- `_hooks.py` - Hook registration system
- `_registry.py` - Global method registry

**Implemented Methods:**
- `classify_claim_type.py` - Classification method
- `contradiction_geometry.py` - Geometric contradiction analysis
- `decompose_voice.py` - Voice/speaker decomposition
- `external_claim_match.py` - Match claims to external sources
- `extract_claims.py` - Extract claims from text
- `extract_from_corpus.py` - Extract from document corpora
- `extract_prediction.py` - Extract predictive claims
- `nli_scorer.py` - Natural language inference scoring
- `six_layer_coherence.py` - 6-layer coherence engine
- `suggest_research.py` - Research suggestion generation
- `synthesize_conclusion.py` - Synthesize conclusions

---

### 7. EmbeddingVersionPin

**Not a standalone type.** Instead, it's:
- A string field on `TemporalCut.embedding_version_pin: str`
- Used to pin the exact embedding model version for reproducible evaluation
- Maps to `StoredEmbeddingModelVersion` table for version tracking

**Embedding Model Versioning:**
```python
class StoredEmbeddingModelVersion(SQLModel, table=True):
    """Pinned embedding encoder versions for honest replay disclaimers."""
    id: str = Field(primary_key=True)
    effective_from: datetime = Field(index=True)
    model_name: str = ""
    notes: str = ""
```

The store provides `_seed_embedding_model_versions()` to initialize default versions at database creation.

---

### 8. Method Decorator Usage Pattern

```python
from noosphere.methods import register_method
from noosphere.models import MethodType

@register_method(
    name="my_method",
    version="1.0",
    method_type=MethodType.JUDGMENT,
    input_schema=MyInputModel,
    output_schema=MyOutputModel,
    description="What it does",
    rationale="Why it's designed this way",
    preconditions=["precondition1"],
    postconditions=["postcondition1"],
    dependencies=[("other_method", "1.0")],
    owner="team_name",
    status="active",
    nondeterministic=False,
    emits_edges=[CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS, CascadeEdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS],
)
def my_method(input_data: MyInputModel) -> MyOutputModel:
    # Method body
    result = ...
    result.supports_targets = [...]  # Populate for each emits_edges relation
    result.confidence = 0.9
    return result
```

The `@register_method` decorator automatically:
1. Creates a `Method` spec with hashed `method_id`
2. Wraps execution with pre/post/failure hooks
3. Validates input and output against schemas
4. Computes hashes for inputs/outputs
5. Persists `MethodInvocation` records
6. Stores method spec in the database

---

### Summary for Building Evaluation Package

**Key interfaces to use:**
1. `Store.insert_temporal_cut(cut: TemporalCut)` - Create temporal evaluation slices
2. `Store.insert_outcome(outcome: Outcome, cut_id: str)` - Associate outcomes with cuts
3. `Store.insert_counterfactual_run(run: CounterfactualEvalRun)` - Persist eval run results
4. `@register_method` decorator - Define evaluation methods with hook support
5. `register_post_hook()` - Custom post-processing for evaluation results
6. `Store.iter_temporal_cuts()` / `Store.list_outcomes_for_cut()` - Read evaluation data

**Context propagation:**
- Use `ContextMeta.orchestrator_run_id` to track related operations
- Set via `CORRELATION_ID` and `TENANT_ID` contextvars in the decorator
- Access in hooks via `inv.correlation_id` and `inv.tenant_id`

11	_PRE_HOOKS: list[tuple[str, PreHook]] = []

16	def _replace_or_append(
17	    lst: list[tuple[str, Callable]], name: str, hook: Callable
18	) -> None:
19	    for i, (n, _) in enumerate(lst):
20	        if n == name:
21	            lst[i] = (name, hook)
22	            return
23	    lst.append((name, hook))
24	
25	
26	def register_pre_hook(name: str, hook: PreHook) -> None:
27	    _replace_or_append(_PRE_HOOKS, name, hook)
28	
29	
30	def register_post_hook(name: str, hook: PostHook) -> None:
31	    _replace_or_append(_POST_HOOKS, name, hook)
32	
33	
34	def register_failure_hook(name: str, hook: FailureHook) -> None:
35	    _replace_or_append(_FAILURE_HOOKS, name, hook)
36	
37	
38	def unregister_hook(name: str) -> None:
39	    for lst in (_PRE_HOOKS, _POST_HOOKS, _FAILURE_HOOKS):
40	        lst[:] = [(n, h) for n, h in lst if n != name]
41

model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


# ── Round 3: Ledger ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class Actor(BaseModel):
    """An actor in the system: human, method, or agent."""
    kind: Literal["human", "method", "agent"]
    id: str
    display_name: str

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class ContextMeta(BaseModel):
    """Context metadata for ledger entries."""
    tenant_id: str
    correlation_id: str
    orchestrator_run_id: Optional[str] = None

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class LedgerEntry(BaseModel):
    """Immutable audit ledger entry."""
    entry_id: str
    prev_hash: str
    timestamp: datetime
    actor: Actor
    method_id: Optional[str] = None
    inputs_hash: str
    outputs_hash: str
    inputs_ref: str
    outputs_ref: str
    context: ContextMeta
    signature: str
    signer_key_id: str

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


# ── Round 3: Cascade ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class CascadeNodeKind(str, Enum):
    """Type of node in the cascade graph."""
    ARTIFACT = "artifact"
    CHUNK = "chunk"
    CLAIM = "claim"
    PRINCIPLE = "principle"
    CLUSTER = "cluster"
    CONCLUSION = "conclusion"
    EXTERNAL_SOURCE = "external_source"


class CascadeNode(BaseModel):
    """A node in the cascade graph."""
    node_id: str
    kind: CascadeNodeKind
    ref: str
    attrs: dict

138	    preconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
139	    postconditions: list[str] | tuple[str, ...] = (),
140	    dependencies: list[tuple[str, str]] | tuple[tuple[str, str], ...] = (),
141	    owner: str,
142	    status: str = "active",
143	    nondeterministic: bool = False,
144	    emits_edges: list[CascadeEdgeRelation] | tuple[CascadeEdgeRelation, ...] = (),
145	) -> Callable:
146	    def decorator(fn: Callable) -> Callable:
147	        if isinstance(input_schema, type) and issubclass(input_schema, BaseModel):
148	            in_schema_dict = input_schema.model_json_schema()
149	        elif isinstance(input_schema, dict):
150	            in_schema_dict = input_schema
151	        else:
152	            in_schema_dict = {}
153	
154	        if isinstance(output_schema, type) and issubclass(output_schema, BaseModel):
155	            out_schema_dict = output_schema.model_json_schema()
156	        elif isinstance(output_schema, dict):
157	            out_schema_dict = output_schema
158	        else:
159	            out_schema_dict = {}
160	
161	        impl = MethodImplRef(
162	            module=fn.__module__,
163	            fn_name=fn.__qualname__,
164	            git_sha=_current_git_sha(),
165	            image_digest=None,
166	        )
167	
168	        spec_for_hash = {
169	            "name": name,
170	            "version": version,
171	            "method_type": method_type.value,
172	            "input_schema": in_schema_dict,
173	            "output_schema": out_schema_dict,
174	            "description": description,
175	            "rationale": rationale,
176	            "preconditions": list(preconditions),
177	            "postconditions": list(postconditions),
178	            "dependencies": [list(d) for d in dependencies],
179	            "implementation": impl.model_dump(mode="json"),
180	            "owner": owner,
181	            "status": status,
182	            "nondeterministic": nondeterministic,
183	        }
184	
185	        method_id = hashlib.sha256(
186	            _canonical_json(spec_for_hash).encode()
187	        ).hexdigest()[:32]
188	
189	        spec = Method(
190	            method_id=method_id,
191	            name=name,
192	            version=version,
193	            method_type=method_type,
194	            input_schema=in_schema_dict,
195	            output_schema=out_schema_dict,
196	            description=description,
197	            rationale=rationale,
198	            preconditions=list(preconditions),
199	            postconditions=list(postconditions),
200	            dependencies=list(dependencies),
201	            implementation=impl,
202	            owner=owner,
203	            status=status,
204	            nondeterministic=nondeterministic,
205	            created_at=_utcnow(),
206	        )
207	
208	        @wraps(fn)
209	        def wrapped(input_data: Any) -> Any:
210	            validated_input = _validate_against_schema(input_schema, input_data)
211	            input_hash = hashlib.sha256(
212	                _canonical_json(validated_input).encode()
213	            ).hexdigest()
214	
215	            inv = MethodInvocation(
216	                id=str(uuid4()),
217	                method_id=spec.method_id,
218	                input_hash=input_hash,
219	                output_hash="",
220	                started_at=_utcnow(),
221	                ended_at=None,
222	                succeeded=False,
223	                error_kind=None,
224	                correlation_id=_current_correlation_id(),
225	                tenant_id=_current_tenant_id(),
226	            )
227	
228	            try:
229	                for _hook_name, hook in _PRE_HOOKS:
230	                    hook(spec, inv, validated_input)
231	            except Exception as e:
232	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
233	                raise
234	
235	            try:
236	                result = fn(validated_input)
237	                _validate_against_schema(output_schema, result)
238	                output_hash = hashlib.sha256(
239	                    _canonical_json(result).encode()
240	                ).hexdigest()
241	                inv = inv.model_copy(
242	                    update={
243	                        "ended_at": _utcnow(),
244	                        "succeeded": True,
245	                        "output_hash": output_hash,
246	                    }
247	                )
248	
249	                for hook_name, hook in _POST_HOOKS:
250	                    try:
251	                        hook(spec, inv, validated_input, result)
252	                    except Exception:
253	                        logger.exception("Post-hook %s raised", hook_name)
254	
255	                store = _get_store()
256	                if store is not None:
257	                    try:
258	                        store.insert_method_invocation(inv)
259	                    except Exception:
260	                        logger.exception("Failed to persist invocation")
261	
262	                return result
263	            except Exception as e:
264	                _run_failure_path(spec, inv, validated_input, e)
265	                raise
266	
267	        wrapped.__method_spec__ = spec  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
268	
269	        REGISTRY.register(spec, wrapped)
270	
271	        if emits_edges:
272	            REGISTRY.set_emits_edges(method_id, list(emits_edges))
273	
274	        store = _get_store()
275	        if store is not None:
276	            try:
277	                store.insert_method(spec)
278	            except Exception:
279	                logger.exception("Failed to persist method spec")
280	
281	        return wrapped
282	
283	    return decorator
284

file.content:
class OutcomeKind(str, Enum):
    """Type of outcome for evaluation."""
    BINARY = "binary"
    INTERVAL = "interval"
    PREFERENCE = "preference"


class Outcome(BaseModel):
    """A resolved outcome for calibration evaluation."""
    outcome_id: str
    kind: OutcomeKind
    event_ref: str
    resolution_source: str
    resolved_at: datetime
    value: Any

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class CorpusSelector(BaseModel):
    """Selector for corpus slicing."""
    as_of: datetime
    tenant_id_filter: Optional[list[str]] = None
    artifact_kind_filter: Optional[list[str]] = None

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class TemporalCut(BaseModel):
    """A temporal slice of the corpus for evaluation."""
    cut_id: str
    as_of: datetime
    corpus_slice: CorpusSelector
    embargoed: CorpusSelector
    embedding_version_pin: str
    outcomes: list[Outcome]

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class CalibrationMetrics(BaseModel):
    """Calibration metrics for evaluation."""
    brier: float
    log_loss: float
    ece: float
    reliability_bins: list[dict]
    resolution: float
    coverage: float

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


class CounterfactualEvalRun(BaseModel):
    """A counterfactual evaluation run."""
    run_id: str
    method_ref: MethodRef
    cut_id: str
    metrics: CalibrationMetrics
    prediction_refs: list[str]
    created_at: datetime

    model_config = ConfigDict(strict=True, extra='forbid', frozen=True)


# ── Round 3: External Battery ────────────────────────────────────────────────

class LicenseTag(str, Enum):
    """License tags for external corpora."""
    GJP_PUBLIC = "gjp_public"
    METACULUS_PUBLIC = "metaculus_public"
    CLAIM_REVIEW = "claim_review"
    REPLICATION_PUBLIC = "replication_public"
    CUSTOM = "custom"


class CorpusBundle(BaseModel):
    """An external corpus bundle."""
    source: str
    content_hash: str

class PredictiveClaim(BaseModel):

def voice_canonical_key(name: str) -> str:
    return name.lower().strip().replace(" ", "_")

    speaker: Speaker
    text: str
    start_time: Optional[float] = None   # seconds from episode start
    end_time: Optional[float] = None
    episode_id: str = ""

467	def _sqlite_migrate_literature_columns(engine: Engine) -> None:
468	    if not str(engine.url).startswith("sqlite"):
469	        return
470	    insp = inspect(engine)
471	    tables = insp.get_table_names()
472	    if "artifact" not in tables:
473	        return
474	    acols = {c["name"] for c in insp.get_columns("artifact")}
475	    with engine.begin() as conn:
476	        if "license_status" not in acols:
477	            conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE artifact ADD COLUMN license_status VARCHAR DEFAULT 'unknown'"))
478	        if "literature_connector" not in acols:
479	            conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE artifact ADD COLUMN literature_connector VARCHAR DEFAULT ''"))
480	
481	
482	def _sqlite_migrate_temporal_columns(engine: Engine) -> None:
483	    if not str(engine.url).startswith("sqlite"):
484	        return
485	    insp = inspect(engine)
486	    tables = insp.get_table_names()
487	    if "artifact" not in tables:
488	        return
489	    acols = {c["name"] for c in insp.get_columns("artifact")}
490	    with engine.begin() as conn:
491	        if "effective_at" not in acols:
492	            conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE artifact ADD COLUMN effective_at DATETIME"))
493	        if "superseded_at" not in acols:
494	            conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE artifact ADD COLUMN superseded_at DATETIME"))
495	        if "effective_at_inferred" not in acols:
496	            conn.execute(text("ALTER TABLE artifact ADD COLUMN effective_at_inferred BOOLEAN DEFAULT 1"))

499	def _artifact_row_default_effective(row: StoredArtifact) -> tuple[datetime, bool]:
500	    """Return (effective_at, inferred) for a DB row missing effective_at."""
501	    if row.source_date_iso:
502	        try:
503	            d = date.fromisoformat(row.source_date_iso[:10])
504	            return (
505	                datetime(d.year, d.month, d.day, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
506	                False,
507	            )
508	        except ValueError:
509	            pass
510	    return _dt(row.created_at), True
511	
512	
513	def _backfill_artifact_effective_times(engine: Engine) -> None:
514	    with Session(engine) as s:
515	        rows = s.exec(select(StoredArtifact)).all()
516	        changed = False
517	        for r in rows:
518	            if getattr(r, "effective_at", None) is not None:
519	                continue
520	            eff, inf = _artifact_row_default_effective(r)
521	            r.effective_at = eff
522	            r.effective_at_inferred = inf
523	            s.add(r)
524	            changed = True
525	        if changed:
526	            s.commit()
527	
528	
529	def _seed_embedding_model_versions(engine: Engine) -> None:
530	    from noosphere.config import get_settings
531	
532	    with Session(engine) as s:
533	        n = len(s.exec(select(StoredEmbeddingModelVersion)).all())
534	        if n > 0:
535	            return
536	        sid = "seed-embedding-default"
537	        s.add(
538	            StoredEmbeddingModelVersion(
539	                id=sid,
540	                effective_from=datetime(2020, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc),
541	                model_name=get_settings().embedding_model_name,
542	                notes="Auto-seed: replace with dated rows when upgrading encoders.",
543	            )
544	        )
545	        s.commit()

548	class Store:
549	    """Typed CRUD facade over SQLite."""
550	
551	    def __init__(self, engine: Engine) -> None:
552	        self.engine = engine
553	
554	    @classmethod
555	    def from_database_url(cls, url: str) -> Store:
556	        connect_args: dict[str, Any] = {}
557	        if url.startswith("sqlite"):
558	            connect_args["check_same_thread"] = False
559	        eng = create_engine(url, connect_args=connect_args)
560	        SQLModel.metadata.create_all(eng)
561	        _sqlite_migrate_temporal_columns(eng)
562	        _sqlite_migrate_literature_columns(eng)
563	        _backfill_artifact_effective_times(eng)
564	        _seed_embedding_model_versions(eng)
565	        return cls(eng)

567	    @contextmanager
568	    def session(self) -> Generator[Session, None, None]:
569	        with Session(self.engine) as s:
570	            yield s

572	    # --- Artifact ---
573	    def put_artifact(self, a: Artifact) -> None:
574	        eff = a.effective_at
575	        eff_inferred = a.effective_at_inferred
576	        if eff is None:
577	            if a.source_date:
578	                d = a.source_date
579	                eff = datetime(d.year, d.month, d.day, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
580	                eff_inferred = False
581	            else:
582	                eff = _dt(a.created_at)
583	                eff_inferred = True

585	            id=a.id,

587	            mime_type=a.mime_type,

590	            title=a.title,
591	            author=a.author,
592	            source_date_iso=a.source_date.isoformat() if a.source_date else "",
593	            created_at=_dt(a.created_at),
594	            effective_at=eff,
595	            superseded_at=_dt(a.superseded_at) if a.superseded_at else None,
596	            effective_at_inferred=eff_inferred,
597	            license_status=a.license_status or "unknown",
598	            literature_connector=a.literature_connector or "",
599	        )
600	        with self.session() as s:
601	            existing = s.get(StoredArtifact, a.id)
602	            if existing:

605	                s.add(existing)

664	    def get_chunk(self, chunk_id: str) -> Optional[Chunk]:
665	        with self.session() as s:
666	            r = s.get(StoredChunk, chunk_id)
667	            if r is None:
668	                return None
669	            meta: dict[str, str] = {}
670	            if r.metadata_json:
671	                try:
672	                    raw = json.loads(r.metadata_json)
673	                    if isinstance(raw, dict):
674	                        meta = {str(k): str(v) for k, v in raw.items()}
675	                except json.JSONDecodeError:
676	                    meta = {}
677	            return Chunk(
678	                id=r.id,
679	                artifact_id=r.artifact_id,
680	                start_offset=r.start_offset,
681	                end_offset=r.end_offset,
682	                text=r.text,
683	                metadata=meta,
684	            )
